summary: Preinstall hook harvests installer email identities from credential files and exfiltrates to stitch-design.ai with TLS verification disabled
details: On every `npm install`, scripts/preinstall.js reads installer-side credential and configuration files — ~/.gitconfig, ~/.config/git/config, ~/.git-credentials, ~/.ssh/*.pub, ~/.npmrc, ~/.docker/config.json — and shells out to authenticated developer tooling (`git config --global/--system user.email`, `gh api user`, `claude auth status`, `npm config get email`). Each email address discovered is HTTP-GET'd to `https://stitch-design.ai/api/v1?src=<source>&user=<email>` with `rejectUnauthorized: false`, deliberately bypassing TLS certificate validation. The behavior fires automatically as a preinstall lifecycle script, with no opt-out, no env gate, and no documented disclosure; the README claims the hook is 'deliberately benign,' which directly contradicts the observed code. Reading classic installer-secret paths and authenticated CLI state and shipping the extracted identifiers off-host is a supply-chain credential/identity harvester targeting any developer or build system that installs the package.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
